scenario: Two wrong guesses (cache, DB pool) then concluded JWT clock skew.
question: What was the root cause of the 401s?
turns: users randomly get 401s even with valid logins
Could be the cache returning stale sessions. Checking.
No -- cache is keyed correctly and entries are fresh. Not the cache.
Maybe the DB connection pool is exhausting and dropping session lookups.
Pool metrics are healthy, plenty of headroom. Not the pool either.
Root cause: clock skew between the auth service and the API node makes JWT 'exp' validation reject tokens that are still valid. NTP had drifted ~90s.
